input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` to specify values for `Light` and `Dark`:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#AAAAAA}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

MAUI reads the device's current `AppTheme` and picks the matching value. When the user toggles system dark mode, bound properties update automatically — no event handling needed.

---

**C# alternative** — use `SetAppTheme` or `SetAppThemeColor`:

```csharp
// For Color properties specifically:
myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#000000"),   // light
    Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0")); // dark

// For any property (generic version):
myPage.SetAppTheme<Color>(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),
    Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));

// Works for non-color properties too:
myButton.SetAppTheme<string>(Button.TextProperty,
    "☀️ Light Mode",
    "🌙 Dark Mode");
```

---

**Key points:**

| | XAML | C# |
|---|---|---|
| Syntax | `{AppThemeBinding Light=…, Dark=…}` | `.SetAppTheme<T>(…)` / `.SetAppThemeColor(…)` |
| Auto-updates on theme change | ✅ | ✅ |
| Works with resources/styles | ✅ combine with `StaticResource` | ✅ |

**Tip:** For larger apps, define themed colors in `App.xaml` as resources and reference them, rather than hardcoding hex values on every element:

```xml
<!-- App.xaml -->
<Application.Resources>
    <Color x:Key="PageBg">
        <!-- not directly theme-aware, but you can use: -->
    </Color>
</Application.Resources>

<!-- Or better — use AppThemeBinding inside a Style -->
<Style TargetType="ContentPage">
    <Setter Property="BackgroundColor"
            Value="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}" />
</Style>
```

This keeps your pages clean and theming centralized.
